summary: Completed comprehensive testing of DevGrill AI Interview Platform. Backend APIs mostly functional (9/10 tests passed), frontend UI fully functional with excellent error handling. Critical issue: Gemini AI integration intermittently fails due to service unavailability.

critical_bugs.issue: Gemini AI model 'gemini-2.5-flash' returns 503 Service Unavailable with message 'This model is currently experiencing high demand'
critical_bugs.impact: Blocks core interview functionality - question generation fails

critical_bugs.issue: Answer evaluation also fails when Gemini API is unavailable
critical_bugs.impact: Cannot evaluate user answers or provide feedback

flaky_endpoints.symptoms: Sometimes works, sometimes returns 503 due to Gemini API high demand
flaky_endpoints.workaround: Retry mechanism or fallback to different model

integration_issues.issue: UI handles AI failures gracefully but doesn't show clear error messages to users

passed_tests: Landing page loads with hero section and all category cards
Navbar navigation works (HOME, DASHBOARD, HISTORY, START INTERVIEW)
Setup page - category, tech stack, difficulty, question count selection
Dashboard page shows data and charts
Interview completion flow and summary display
Frontend error handling for API failures

success_percentage.backend: 90% (9/10 APIs working, 1 critical AI integration issue)

action_item_for_main_agent: CRITICAL: Fix Gemini AI integration. The model 'gemini-2.5-flash' is experiencing high demand and returning 503 errors. Consider: 1) Implementing retry logic with exponential backoff, 2) Adding fallback to different Gemini model (e.g., gemini-1.5-flash), 3) Adding user-friendly error messages in UI when AI is unavailable, 4) Consider rate limiting or queueing for AI requests.
